textPlain: I just ran the checksum for the current ISO file of the full Xubuntu desktop version on the Xubuntu website, and the checksum appears to be valid.https://mirror.us.leaseweb.net/ubuntu-cdimage/xubuntu/releas...[user@host]$ lsSHA256SUMS  SHA256SUMS.gpg  xubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso[user@host]$ cat SHA256SUMSb61e083d8a5ab003bad6ef7ea31ec21d7bfdf19b99d75987ab3fa3bbe85ec1bf *xubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso[user@host]$ sha256sum xubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.isob61e083d8a5ab003bad6ef7ea31ec21d7bfdf19b99d75987ab3fa3bbe85ec1bf  xubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso[user@host]$ echo $?0
There's a sticked comment on the source thread: https://old.reddit.com/r/xubuntu/comments/1oa43gt/xubuntuorg...
Let's not kid ourselves. A state level actor who is playing the long game can compromise any distro, package, etc. without us knowing about it.
fortunately, in this case, it seems like the malware may be moot if you use the iso to wipe your windows installation...

That is why I use Qubes OS [1] in order to have a certain peace of mind.[1] https://www.qubes-os.org/EDIT: further comment below:On second thought, Qubes OS does not prevent such types of malicious downloads; it can also happen to Qubes images. Verify your downloads with checksums and cryptographic signatures [2].[2] https://doc.qubes-os.org/en/latest/project-security/verifyin...

From what I understood, it's the torrent link that downloads a compromised zip file rather then the authentic image:"Torrent downloads over at https://xubuntu.org/download/ are serving a zip file with a suspicious exe and a tos.txt inside. The TOS starts with Copyright (c) 2026 Xubuntu.org which is sus, because it is 2025. I opened the .exe with file-roller and couldn't find any .torrent inside."
And where did you get the reference SHA256SUMS from ? Did you check the gpg signature on them against a good sig from somewhere?

> Thanks everyone. We're beholden to our hosting environment for upgrades and it looks like there was a bit of a slip-up here. It's being worked on, but for now the Downloads page is disabled.Calling this a "slip-up" is an outrageous downplay. If anything this makes me suspicious of the moderator who posted the comment too. One does not accidentally prepare a zip file with a malicious exe and xubuntu-specific language, upload it to a server, and point a torrent link at it.
Calling this "a bit of a slip-up" while neither confirming nor denying the presence of malware is weird at best and incredibly suspicious at worst.
That kind of defeatism isn’t helpful.The present case also just seems malware easily detected by VirusTotal: https://old.reddit.com/r/xubuntu/comments/1oa43gt/xubuntuorg...

Jia Tan with the XZ backdoor was caught because some performance obsessed person noticed a tiny delay...  I'm sure they learned their lesson and are ensuring their next backdoor doesn't impact performance.
Sure, but realistically, how many of us right here have state level actors in our threat models? I sure don't, because it'd be impossible to live a normal life then.
Thanks for this link. Opening reddit links on mobile is very frustrating for me because it opens the app and messes with the browser back button for me. Not sure if others have that problem too.

On second thought, Qubes OS does not prevent such types of malicious downloads; it can also happen to Qubes images. Verify your downloads with checksums and cryptographic signatures [1].[1] https://doc.qubes-os.org/en/latest/project-security/verifyin...
qubes is just as vulnerable as xubuntu in this case (poor website security) no?
